activities_projects.title: Character Analysis: Meena’s Resilience
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the internal and external conflicts of the protagonist
steps: Read the story focusing on Meena’s daily struggles and her desire to learn.
Create a T-chart in your notebook: label one side 'Societal Obstacles' and the other 'Meena’s Actions'.
Write a 50-word paragraph explaining how Meena overcomes her fear of the 'black marks' (letters).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze character development and the concept of perseverance.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and accuracy of textual evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Symbolism of the River
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the metaphorical meaning of the river in the story
steps: Identify three specific instances where the river 'speaks' or provides comfort to Meena.
Discuss with a partner: Is the river a real character or a reflection of Meena’s inner thoughts?
Write a short explanation of what the river symbolizes (e.g., freedom, knowledge, or change).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand basic symbolism and abstract themes in literature.
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical interpretation and supportive reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study: Education Barriers
activities_projects.objective: Compare Meena's educational journey with modern-day access
steps: List the specific reasons why Meena was not initially sent to school.
Compare these barriers with the facilities and rights available to children in your city/village today.
Write three sentences suggesting how the village community could have supported Meena better.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literary themes to social reality and the 'Right to Education'.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of comparison and social awareness (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Expansion: Contextual Synonyms
steps: Select 5 challenging words from the chapter (e.g., *scrawled*, *murmur*, *persistent*).
Find two synonyms and one antonym for each word using a dictionary.
Compose a short paragraph of 4 sentences using at least 3 of these new words in a different setting.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students master nuanced vocabulary usage and contextual application.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct usage of synonyms and creative integration (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Persuasive Writing: A Letter to Parents
activities_projects.objective: Practice persuasive writing from a character's perspective
steps: Imagine you are the teacher or a supportive elder in Meena's village.
Write a formal letter to Meena’s parents persuading them to let her attend school regularly.
Include at least three benefits of literacy that would help Meena’s future.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop persuasive and formal writing skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of arguments and appropriateness of tone (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Atmospheric Description: Show, Don't Tell
activities_projects.objective: Use imagery to recreate the story's setting
steps: Select the scene where Meena sits by the riverbank.
Rewrite that scene in 5 sentences focusing on sensory details (sounds of water, heat of the sun, texture of the mud).
Avoid using the word 'beautiful'; instead, show why it is beautiful through your description.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve creative writing and the use of vivid imagery.
activities_projects.evaluation: Effective use of sensory details and adjectives (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dialogue Scripting: The Turning Point
activities_projects.objective: Convert a narrative climax into a dramatic script
steps: Choose the moment Meena decides she will no longer be afraid of learning.
Rewrite this scene as a play script, including 'stage directions' in brackets (e.g., [Meena looks at the water with determination]).
Role-play the script with a classmate.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn script-writing formats and dramatic expression.
activities_projects.evaluation: Format accuracy and emotional resonance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Grammar: Direct to Indirect Speech
activities_projects.objective: Transform dialogues from the text into reported speech
steps: Identify 5 sentences of direct speech (dialogue) from the chapter.
Convert them into indirect (reported) speech in your notebook.
Circle the changes you made to the tenses and pronouns.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply grammatical rules of narration to literary text.

activities_projects.title: Alternative Perspective: The River's Diary
activities_projects.objective: Explore the narrative from a non-human viewpoint
steps: Write a diary entry from the perspective of the River.
Describe what the River thinks of Meena’s daily visits and her secret efforts to learn.
Reflect on the 'changes' the River sees in Meena's confidence over time.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy and practice shifting narrative point-of-view.
activities_projects.evaluation: Consistency of voice and creative insight (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Thematic Project: 'Learning Together'
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize the chapter's theme with the unit title
steps: In small groups, create a poster titled 'Learning Together'.
Include drawings or quotes from the story that show how Meena learned from nature, herself, and others.
Explain how the title of the unit fits this specific story.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students synthesize complex themes through collaboration and visual art.
